attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who bridges to early splits and times late kicks on technical finishes, particularly effective in reduced sprint scenarios from breakaways rather than solo attacks.
team_tactics: Team (CCC historically) sets climbing tempo to position him for selective finishes; willing to form pragmatic alliances in breaks to prevent GC-motivated stalling, as seen with Hirschi and Van Avermaet.
terrain_preference: Technical cobbled finishes where bike handling and positioning matter; excels in reduced sprint scenarios from breakaways. Struggles when race splits on climbs or requires sustained acceleration to match pure climbers.
vulnerability: Crash-prone in nervous peloton phases (3 crashes recorded across Flanders races); fails to follow explosive accelerations from pure climbers like Alaphilippe; fades in long-distance efforts after bridging.
key_patterns: Crashes repeatedly in Flemish Classics nervous phases: 2017, 2018, 2022 incidents documented.
Wins from small breakaway sprints on technical terrain, beating quality sprinters like Van Avermaet.
Bridges to early splits showing aggressive intent but consistently fades in race finales.
Effective second-wheel positioning in breakaways, tracking key moves like Bernard's Mont Brouilly attack.
Team sets sustained climbing tempo to deliver him to selective finishes rather than explosive moves.
form_trajectory: negative: Most recent 2022 data shows hard crash from bunch positioning error; 2020-2021 showed inability to reach podium groups despite following moves, suggesting declining explosive power.
